vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1084

narratives.Overview:
## Parent / Predecessor

- Carved out of #1072 during the 2026-05-12 supply-chain cohort refinement.
- Blocked-by #11 (Proposal: Publish Deft as an NPM + PIP CLI). PyPI OIDC trusted-publishing is meaningless until deft is actually publishing to PyPI, which #11 owns.

## What to build

PyPI OIDC trusted-publishing workflow for the deft CLI package. Replaces the static-token publish path proposed in #11 with a short-lived OIDC token issued by GitHub Actions, eliminating the long-lived `PYPI_API_TOKEN` secret-handling surface.

Specifically:

- New `.github/workflows/release-pypi.yml` workflow:
  - Trigger: `release: types: [published]` so it fires after `task release:publish -- <ver>` flips the GitHub release public.
  - Permissions: top-level `permissions: contents: read`; per-job `permissions: id-token: write, contents: read`.
  - Uses `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@<sha>` (pinned per #1072) -- no `password:` / `PYPI_API_TOKEN` input.
- PyPI-side registration (HITL): one-time human step at `https://pypi.org/manage/account/publishing/` registering deftai/directive as a trusted publisher for the deft package name (workflow filename + ref pattern + environment).
- Dry-run path: `release: types: [prereleased]` or a manual `workflow_dispatch` against TestPyPI to verify the OIDC flow before any production publish.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] #11 has landed (deft is published to PyPI at some package name)
- [ ] `release-pypi.yml` workflow exists with `id-token: write` permission and `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@<sha>` (no `password:` input)
- [ ] PyPI trusted-publisher registration completed in the PyPI web UI for the registered package name
- [ ] A TestPyPI publish succeeds end-to-end via the OIDC path (dry-run verification)
- [ ] A production publish succeeds at the next release cut
- [ ] `task check` passes

## Type

HITL -- the PyPI-side trusted-publisher registration is a one-time human web-UI step.

## Blocked by

- Blocked by #11 (deft must actually be on PyPI before there is anything to OIDC-publish to)

## Refs

- #1069 (parent supply-chain audit umbrella)
- #1072 (predecessor: Actions SHA-pinning + permissions hardening; carved this out so #1072 can ship as fully AFK)
- #11 (NPM + PIP distribution proposal -- the upstream dependency)

references.title: Issue #1084: chore(security): PyPI OIDC trusted-publishing workflow (blocked-by #11)
